title: accumulator.rs: AnthropicToolAccumulator finalize doesn't validate JSON completeness — partial tool_calls leak to dispatch
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

**Dimension:** Functional Correctness

**Location:** src/tools/accumulator.rs lines 64-82 (ToolCallAccumulator::finalize), 273-293 (to_openai_tool_calls_json), 36-39 (process_delta slot growth).

**Violation:**
(1) finalize filters only by !id.is_empty() && !function_name.is_empty(). An arguments string that was streamed as '{"path":' (and never completed) passes through. tools/mod.rs:805 from_str::<HashMap<String,Value>>(&args) falls back to unwrap_or_default() — a broken tool call silently becomes an empty-args invocation, producing confusing error messages downstream.
(2) to_openai_tool_calls_json sets 'arguments': input_json as STRING without validating that input_json is well-formed JSON. Upstream Anthropic provider bugs or stream cancellation mid-delta produce bad JSON.
(3) process_delta slot growth is unbounded: 'while self.tool_calls.len() <= index { push(default) }' — an out-of-range index from upstream allocates a huge Vec.

**Mandated Refactor:**
(1) Validate serde_json::from_str::<Value>(&input_json) at finalize; return ToolError::MalformedToolCall if it fails.
(2) Cap index to 100; reject deltas beyond.
(3) For Anthropic-shaped serialization, keep 'input' as Value rather than double-serializing.
